2604.15774.title: MemEvoBench: Benchmarking Memory MisEvolution in LLM Agents
2604.15774.paper_summary: Equipping Large Language Models (LLMs) with persistent memory enhances interaction continuity and personalization but introduces new safety risks. Specifically, contaminated or biased memory accumulation can trigger abnormal agent behaviors. Existing evaluation methods have not yet established a standardized framework for measuring memory misevolution. This phenomenon refers to the gradual behavioral drift resulting from repeated exposure to misleading information. To address this gap, we introduce MemEvoBench, the first benchmark evaluating long-horizon memory safety in LLM agents against adversarial memory injection, noisy tool outputs, and biased feedback. The framework consists of QA-style tasks across 7 domains and 36 risk types, complemented by workflow-style tasks adapted from 20 Agent-SafetyBench environments with noisy tool returns. Both settings employ mixed benign and misleading memory pools within multi-round interactions to simulate memory evolution. Experiments on representative models reveal substantial safety degradation under biased memory updates. Our analysis suggests that memory evolution is a significant contributor to these failures. Furthermore, static prompt-based defenses prove insufficient, underscoring the urgency of securing memory evolution in LLM agents.
2604.15774.paper_summary_zh: 為大型語言模型（LLMs）配備持久記憶可以增強互動的連貫性和個性化，但同時也引入了新的安全風險。<lb/>具體而言，受到污染或偏見的記憶累積可能會引發異常的代理行為。<lb/>現有的評估方法尚未建立標準化的框架來測量記憶的錯誤演變。<lb/>這一現象指的是由於反覆接觸誤導性資訊而導致的行為逐漸偏移。<lb/>為了解決這一問題，我們推出了MemEvoBench，這是第一個基準，評估LLM代理在對抗性記憶注入、噪聲工具輸出和偏見反饋下的長期記憶安全性。<lb/>該框架由7個領域和36種風險類型的QA風格任務組成，並輔以從20個Agent-SafetyBench環境中改編的工作流程風格任務，這些環境具有噪聲工具的返回。<lb/>這兩種設置在多輪互動中使用混合的良性和誤導性記憶池，以模擬記憶的演變。<lb/>對代表性模型的實驗顯示，在偏見記憶更新下，安全性顯著下降。<lb/>我們的分析表明，記憶演變是這些失敗的重要原因。此外，靜態提示基礎的防禦措施被證明不足，凸顯了保護LLM代理記憶演變的緊迫性。
2604.15774.author: Weiwei Xie et.al.
2604.15774.authors: Weiwei Xie, Shaoxiong Guo, Fan Zhang, Tian Xia, Xue Yang, Lizhuang Ma, Junchi Yan, Qibing Ren
